## Ogment AI

Launched this week

Your AI coworker, in Slack. Just tag @O.

@O is the ultimate AI coworker that lives natively in Slack. Tag @O like a colleague, to ask anything or delegate daily tasks in plain English. It connects to 1,000+ tools your business runs on, does work while you sleep, and shares memory and skills across your whole team right in Slack, on any model you choose, including your own. One-click install, and everyone is AI-enabled in under 5min, not just your power users. Zero friction, maximum adoption.

## Ogment AI

Launched this week [...] @O - AI coworker in Slack

Maker

📌

Hey Product Hunt 👋 I'm Teo, CEO and co-founder of Ogment.

Most companies are told to "adopt AI", but in practice that means asking non-technical people to learn new apps, prompt engineering, and workflow builders. There's one thing we've learned over the past two years offering AI to companies: any bit of friction kills adoption. A few power users push through, true, but everyone else gets left behind.

You've probably seen the wave of Slack-native agents shipping lately, Claude Tag included. They're strong for technical teams who want to own the setup. We went the other way: every employee gets their own AI coworker, on any model you choose (including your own local LLM), with zero setup.

results.title: Ogment AI | LinkedIn
results.content:
# Ogment AI
Your AI coworker, in Slack.
Technology, Information and Internet • San Mateo, CA • 855 followers • 11-50 employees [...] Your AI Coworker, in Slack. Ogment gives every employee a personal AI agent that lives right inside Slack - no new app to learn, no technical skills required. Anyone can create their own agent, connect their tools, and start delegating real work in plain English. Ask it to pull data from your CRM, turn a thread into a Linear ticket, summarize last week's tickets every Monday morning, or connect a new tool - and it does the work, not just talk about it. The agent is built for Slack, engineered to remove every bit of friction so the whole team actually uses it for maximum adoption. Ogment puts that power in the hands of the people closest to the work - the sales rep, the ops manager, the support lead - so no one waits in backlog to automate their tasks. The result: an organization where [...] love AI agents in Slack. No tool switching, context flows naturally, magic happens in threads. Here's why this approach wins! Subscribe for workplace AI tips 💬 #SlackAI #workplacetech #AIadoption [Likes: 2, Comments: 0]

results.title: AI Is Single-Player. Your Work Isn't. - by KP
results.content:
One line got me hooked

When Teo, CEO of Ogment AI, walked me through his product, one sentence stopped me:

“Using an AI agent should be as easy as tagging a colleague.”

That’s the whole product. No new app to learn. No prompt engineering. No workflow builders. You tag @O in Slack, in plain English, and it gets to work.

The private notebook becomes a team whiteboard

@O is an AI coworker that lives natively in Slack. But the part that made me sit up: it shares memory and skills across your entire team.

When one person teaches it something, everyone benefits. Lessons compound. Context builds. The whole team gets smarter together. [...] # Build In Public Newsletter

# AI Is Single-Player. Your Work Isn't.

### Meet Ogment: the AI coworker your whole team shares. Lives in Slack. Compounds daily.

KP

Jul 08, 2026

One chat window. One context. One user.

That’s how almost every AI tool works today. Your marketer has her own AI conversations. Your engineer has his. Your ops lead has hers. Fifty employees, fifty private notebooks. Nothing shared. Nothing compounding.

Meanwhile, actual work is a team sport. It happens in threads, channels, and shared projects. Mostly in Slack.

That gap, between where AI lives and where work lives, is the biggest unsolved problem in workplace AI.

This week I found the team closing it.

One line got me hooked [...] And @O doesn’t just answer questions. It connects to 1,000+ tools your business already runs on (Gmail, Salesforce, Notion, Linear, Stripe) and actually executes the work. Set up automations in plain English and they run while you sleep.

Why I’m backing the small team

Yes, the giants are shipping Slack agents too. They’re strong products.

But model companies are running many races at once. Frontier models. APIs. Consumer apps. Coding tools. A Slack coworker is one bet among dozens.

For Ogment, it’s the only bet. Every sprint, every roadmap call, every support ticket serves this single product. History rewards that kind of maniacal focus. Figma out-obsessed Adobe. Superhuman out-crafted Gmail.
